search results, FocusPanel scenario rows, and IssueCard all route via the shared address chain to canonical #/evals and #/issues detail.

re-measure (address.js session-eval kind + generic-arm change): video of the full chain — palette scenario row → #/evals/session-console/launcher-picker-shows-harness-icon with EventDetail rendered; palette issue row → #/issues/portable-layout-… detail; FocusPanel .fp-scenario → #/evals/video-evidence/video-plays-in-eval-tab detail; FocusPanel IssueCard → same canonical issue route; none merely focused the node or opened the popup

Palette scenario pick → #/evals/eval-score-badge/count-renders (Evals detail rendered); palette issue pick → #/issues/spec-search-171… (Issues detail); node-info Issues tab IssueCard → #/issues/github%2360. All via addressHash/navigateAddress; none merely focused the node. Scenario-less evalAddress(nodeId) → #/evals?node=<id> covered by the eval-score-badge nav video.

On f822c666: palette scenario pick → #/evals/eval-score-badge/count-renders detail; palette issue pick → #/issues/spec-search-171… detail; IssueCard → #/issues/github#60 detail; stat-bar aggregate → #/evals?q=is:eval state:current node:eval-score-badge — all through addressHash/navigateAddress, the aggregate minted by nodeEvalQuery.
re-measure after detailBackHash change: palette scenario→eval detail, palette issue→issue detail, NodeView IssueCard→issue detail — all land on canonical routes

Re-measure after the Dashboard.jsx overlay-guard change: palette scenario pick lands #/evals/terminal-input/chrome-clicks-keep-tui-focus with the detail rendered; palette issue pick lands #/issues/<id> with the thread rendered; the dispatch node popup's Issues tab IssueCard routes to canonical #/issues/github#12; a session-scoped eval detail's back anchor lands #/evals?q=is:eval scope:<full-id>, and that list's one session anchor (terminal door) lands #/sessions/<id>. Transcript alongside; note: scoping the q token with a truncated id prefix yields a truncated door that falls back to #/sessions/new — measured with the canonical full-id scope the chain is exact.
